summary: Corrected plbonus .data symbol boundaries/order in GALE01 symbols.txt by splitting two coarse data objects into six string labels before the existing jumptables, and changed setKindToOne_inline from static inline to static in src/melee/pl/plbonus.c. The decomp.dev report says this produced 3 new full matches in main/melee/pl/plbonus and improved matched code by +1072 bytes.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, replaced broad lbl_803BCF20 size 0x54 and lbl_803BCF74 size 0x4C object entries with more precise string entries at lbl_803BCF20, lbl_803BCF2C, lbl_803BCF48, lbl_803BCF74, lbl_803BCFA0, and lbl_803BCFB0.
Marked the newly split labels as data:string with sizes 0xA, 0x1A, 0x2B, 0x2C, 0x10, and 0xE respectively.
Preserved the following jumptable symbols at 0x803BCFC0, 0x803BD014, and 0x803BD0B0 after the string region.
In src/melee/pl/plbonus.c, changed setKindToOne_inline(int player, int kind) from static inline to static.
smart_moves: The symbols.txt adjustment did not just rename data; it refined object boundaries so the data section reflects separate strings instead of two oversized anonymous blobs.
Splitting strings before jumptable_803BCFC0 likely fixed data ordering/alignment pressure around plbonus jump tables without touching unrelated code.
Removing the inline qualifier from setKindToOne_inline is a minimal source-level matching lever: it changes compiler emission behavior while leaving the function body and call semantics intact.
The PR kept the fix narrow: only the symbol map and one storage/inline specifier in plbonus.c changed.
decomp_lessons: When a file is nearly matching but remains off by tiny amounts, inspect nearby .data symbol boundaries; oversized data objects can hide multiple strings and disturb data ordering.
For Melee GALE01 symbols, string data should be represented as individual labels with exact sizes and data:string annotations when those boundaries are known.

Inline qualifiers can affect matching independently of function logic. If a helper named inline does not match as inline, try matching the original compiler behavior with static/non-inline rather than rewriting the body.
Data-ordering fixes around string pools and jumptables can unlock code matches, as shown here by three plbonus functions becoming 100% matched.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data labels use address-based lbl_803Bxxxx names.
Jump tables use address-based jumptable_803Bxxxx names and scope:local in symbols.txt.
Known plbonus globals/functions retain pl_ prefixes, such as pl_803BCE70 and pl_80038788.
The helper name setKindToOne_inline retained its existing suffix even after the C declaration became static rather than static inline; the PR does not provide evidence for a rename.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split coarse .data objects into exact string-sized labels to correct section layout.
Annotate symbol-map strings with data:string so the linker/symbol tooling treats the region as separate string objects.
Use a small C declaration change, static inline to static, to alter compiler inlining/emission and recover matches.
Bot results reported 3 new matches: pl_80038788, pl_8003906C, and pl_80039450 in main/melee/pl/plbonus.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 40.78%, +0.03% and +1072 bytes.
decomp-dev bot reported pl_80038788 improved from 99.97% to 100.00%, pl_8003906C from 99.98% to 100.00%, and pl_80039450 from 99.97% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that split broad .data objects into data:string labels near jumptables.
Search for setKindToOne_inline history and whether the name should eventually drop the _inline suffix.
Inspect remaining plbonus near-matches for unresolved string pool or jumptable boundary issues.
Search for decomp-dev reports where removing inline produced full matches.
